    Filter Effects

    UITK Effects provides Filter Function Definition assets for richer visual effects in UI Toolkit.

    Each filter works with labels, images, and vector graphics. Apply the same filter multiple times or chain different filters to build a composite effect on a single element.

    Filter stacking order

    When stacking multiple filters on one element, use the following order from top to bottom in UI Builder's Filter list:

    1. Inner Shadow
    2. Outline
    3. Outer Glow
    4. Drop Shadow

    Each filter processes the output of the previous stage, so changing the order can alter the final appearance even when the same filters and parameters are used. Use the recommended order unless you are intentionally creating a different look.

    Caution

    A different order may cause outlines, glows, or shadows to spread farther than expected or distort the intended shape.

    Available filters

    • Drop Shadow: Adds a shadow shape behind text or images, making it look as if the shadow is cast by light.
    • Inner Shadow: Adds a shadow along the inner edge of an object, making the surface look recessed or indented.
    • Outer Glow: Makes light appear to spread outward from text or images.
    • Outline: Draws one or more outlines with Outside, Center, or Inside alignment.
